name: workflows-nodes-base.code
description: Run custom JavaScript or Python code

options.name: Run Once for All Items
options.description: Run this code only once, no matter how many input items there are

options.name: Run Once for Each Item
options.description: Run this code as many times as there are input items

properties.name: jsCode

values.description: Input will be split in batches to throttle requests. -1 for disabled. 0 will be treated as 1.

options.name: allowUnauthorizedCerts
options.description: Whether to download the response even if SSL certificate validation is not possible

options.name: No Brackets

options.name: Brackets Only

values.description: Max number of redirects to follow

values.name: neverError
values.description: Whether to succeeds also when status code is not 2xx

values.name: responseFormat
values.description: The format in which the data gets returned from the URL

values.name: outputPropertyName

values.name: paginationMode

name: workflows-nodes-base.itemLists
description: Helper for working with lists of items and transforming arrays

options.name: Concatenate Items
options.description: Combine fields into a list in a single new item

options.name: Limit
options.description: Remove items if there are too many

options.name: Remove Duplicates
options.description: Remove extra items that are similar

options.name: Sort
options.description: Change the item order

options.name: Split Out Items
options.description: Turn a list or values of object's properties inside item(s) into separate items

options.name: Summarize
options.description: Aggregate items together (pivot table)

options.name: All Item Data (Into a Single List)

values.name: fieldToAggregate
values.description: The name of a field in the input items to aggregate together
values.hint: Enter the field name as text

values.name: renameField
values.description: Whether to give the field a different name in the output

values.name: outputFieldName
values.description: The name of the field to put the aggregated data in. Leave blank to use the input field name.

properties.name: destinationFieldName
properties.description: The name of the output field to put the data in

options.name: disableDotNotation
options.description: Whether to disallow referencing child fields using `parent.child` in the field name

options.name: mergeLists
options.description: Whether to merge the output into a single flat list (rather than a list of lists), if the field to aggregate is a list

options.name: includeBinaries
options.description: Whether to include the binary data in the new item

options.name: keepOnlyUnique
options.description: Whether to keep only unique binaries by comparing mime types, file types, file sizes and file extensions

options.name: keepMissing
options.description: Whether to add a null entry to the aggregated list when there is a missing or null value

properties.name: maxItems
properties.description: If there are more items than this number, some are removed

properties.name: keep
properties.description: When removing items, whether to keep the ones at the start or the ending

properties.name: compare
properties.description: The fields of the input items to compare to see if they are the same

properties.description: Fields in the input to exclude from the comparison

properties.name: fieldsToCompare
properties.description: Fields in the input to add to the comparison

options.name: disableDotNotation
options.description: Whether to disallow referencing child fields using `parent.child` in the field name

options.name: removeOtherFields
options.description: Whether to remove any fields that are not being compared. If disabled, will keep the values from the first of the duplicates.

properties.description: The fields of the input items to compare to see if they are the same

values.name: order

This node is where the workflow execution starts (when you click the ‘test’ button on the canvas).

Explore other ways to trigger your workflow (e.g on a schedule, or a webhook)
properties.name: notice

name: workflows-nodes-base.merge
description: Merges data of multiple streams once data from both is available

properties.name: mode

options.name: Append
options.description: Output items of each input, one after the other

options.name: Combine
options.description: Merge matching items together

options.name: SQL Query
options.description: Write a query to do the merge

options.name: Choose Branch
options.description: Output data from a specific branch, without modifying it

properties.description: How input data should be merged

properties.name: combineBy

options.name: Matching Fields
options.description: Combine items with the same field values

options.name: Position
options.description: Combine items based on their order

options.name: All Possible Combinations
options.description: Every pairing of every two items (cross join)

properties.description: How input data should be merged

properties.name: numberInputs
properties.description: The number of data inputs you want to merge. The node waits for all connected inputs to be executed.

values.name: mergeMode

options.name: Deep Merge
options.description: Merge at every level of nesting

options.name: Shallow Merge
options.description: Merge at the top level only (all nested fields will come from the same input)

values.hint: How to merge when there are sub-fields below the top-level ones

values.name: overrideEmpty
values.description: Whether to override the preferred input version for a field if it is empty and the other version isn't. Here 'empty' means undefined, null or an empty string.

options.name: fuzzyCompare
options.description: Whether to tolerate small type differences when comparing fields. E.g. the number 3 and the string '3' are treated as the same.

properties.displayName: Fields To Match Have Different Names

properties.description: Whether name(s) of field to match are different in input 1 and input 2

options.description: All of input 2, with data from input 1 added in (right join)

name: workflows-nodes-base.respondToEvent
description: Inject an instruction into the agent's autonomy room. Use as a single-node workflow to act as a task template, or as a step in a larger workflow to wake the agent in response to an event.

properties.name: instructions
properties.description: What the agent should do when this node fires. The text is injected as a memory in the autonomy room and processed by the AutonomyService loop.

properties.name: displayName
properties.description: Optional friendly label included in the instruction header. Defaults to the node's name when empty.

properties.name: wakeMode

options.name: Inject Now
options.description: Add the instruction to the autonomy room immediately; AutonomyService picks it up on its next cycle.

options.name: Next Autonomy Cycle
options.description: Queue the instruction for the next scheduled autonomy cycle without forcing an immediate wake.

properties.description: How the autonomy loop should handle the injected instruction.

description: Triggers the workflow on a given schedule

properties.displayName:
This workflow will run on the schedule you define here once you activate it.

For testing, you can also trigger it manually: by going back to the canvas and clicking 'execute workflow'
properties.name: notice

values.name: secondsInterval
values.description: Number of seconds between each workflow trigger
values.hint: Must be in range 1-59

values.name: minutesInterval
values.description: Number of minutes between each workflow trigger
values.hint: Must be in range 1-59

values.name: hoursInterval
values.description: Number of hours between each workflow trigger
values.hint: Must be in range 1-23

values.name: daysInterval
values.description: Number of days between each workflow trigger
values.hint: Must be in range 1-31

values.name: weeksInterval
values.description: Would run every week unless specified otherwise

values.name: monthsInterval
values.description: Would run every month unless specified otherwise

values.name: triggerAtDayOfMonth
values.description: The day of the month to trigger (1-31)
values.hint: If a month doesn’t have this day, the node won’t trigger

values.name: triggerAtHour
values.description: The hour of the day to trigger

values.name: triggerAtMinute
values.description: The minute past the hour to trigger (0-59)

values.displayName: You can find help generating your cron expression here
values.name: notice
expression
values.hint: Format: [Second] [Minute] [Hour] [Day of Month] [Month] [Day of Week]

name: workflows-nodes-base.set
description: Modify, add, or remove item fields

options.name: Manual Mapping
options.description: Edit item fields one by one
options.action: Edit item fields one by one

options.name: JSON
options.description: Customize item output with JSON

properties.name: duplicateItem
properties.description: Whether this item should be duplicated a set number of times

properties.name: duplicateCount
properties.description: How many times the item should be duplicated, mainly used for testing and debugging

properties.displayName: Item duplication is set in the node settings. This option will be ignored when the workflow runs automatically.
properties.name: duplicateWarning
fields
properties.description: Edit existing fields or add new ones to modify the output data

values.name: name
values.description: Name of the field to set the value of. Supports dot-notation. Example: data.person[0].name.

values.name: type
values.description: The field value type

properties.name: include
properties.description: How to select the fields you want to include in your output items

options.name: All Input Fields
options.description: Also include all unchanged fields from the input

options.name: No Input Fields
options.description: Include only the fields specified above

options.name: Selected Input Fields
options.description: Also include the fields listed in the parameter “Fields to Include”

options.name: All Input Fields Except
options.description: Exclude the fields listed in the parameter “Fields to Exclude”

properties.name: includeOtherFields
properties.description: Whether to pass to the output all the input fields (along with the fields set in 'Fields to Set')

properties.name: include
properties.description: How to select the fields you want to include in your output items

options.name: All
options.description: Also include all unchanged fields from the input

options.name: Selected
options.description: Also include the fields listed in the parameter “Fields to Include”

options.name: All Except
options.description: Exclude the fields listed in the parameter “Fields to Exclude”

properties.name: includeFields
properties.description: Comma-separated list of the field names you want to include in the output. You can drag the selected fields from the input panel.

properties.name: excludeFields
properties.description: Comma-separated list of the field names you want to exclude from the output. You can drag the selected fields from the input panel.

properties.name: includeFields
properties.description: Comma-separated list of the field names you want to include in the output. You can drag the selected fields from the input panel.

properties.name: excludeFields
properties.description: Comma-separated list of the field names you want to exclude from the output. You can drag the selected fields from the input panel.

options.name: includeBinary
options.description: Whether binary data should be included if present in the input item

options.name: stripBinary
options.description: Whether binary data should be stripped from the input item. Only applies when "Include Other Input Fields" is enabled.

options.name: ignoreConversionErrors
options.description: Whether to ignore field type errors and apply a less strict type conversion

options.name: dotNotation
options.description: By default, dot-notation is used in property names. This means that "a.b" will set the property "b" underneath "a" so { "a": { "b": value} }. If that is not intended this can be deactivated, it will then set { "a.b": value } instead.

name: workflows-nodes-base.splitInBatches
description: Split data into batches and iterate over each batch

properties.displayName: You may not need this node — workflows nodes automatically run once for each input item. More info
properties.name: splitInBatchesNotice
batchSize
properties.description: The number of items to return with each call

options.name: reset
options.description: Whether the node starts again from the beginning of the input items. This will treat incoming data as a new set rather than continuing with the previous items.

name: workflows-nodes-base.switch
description: Route items depending on defined expression or rules

properties.name: mode

options.name: Rules
options.description: Build a matching rule for each output

options.name: Expression
options.description: Write an expression to return the output index

properties.description: How data should be routed

properties.name: numberOutputs
properties.description: How many outputs to create

properties.name: numberOutputs
properties.description: How many outputs to create

properties.name: output
properties.hint: The index to route the item to, starts at 0
properties.description: The output index to send the input item to. Use an expression to calculate which input item should be routed to which output. The expression must return a number.

values.name: outputKey
values.description: The label of output to which to send data to if rule matches

properties.description: If the type of an expression doesn't match the type of the comparison, workflows will try to cast the expression to the required type. E.g. for booleans "false" or 0 will be cast to false
properties.name: looseTypeValidation

options.name: fallbackOutput
options.description: If no rule matches the item will be sent to this output, by default they will be ignored
Whether to ignore letter case when evaluating conditions
options.name: ignoreCase

options.description: If the type of an expression doesn't match the type of the comparison, workflows will try to cast the expression to the required type. E.g. for booleans "false" or 0 will be cast to false
options.name: looseTypeValidation

options.displayName: Send data to all matching outputs
options.name: allMatchingOutputs
options.description: Whether to send data to all outputs meeting conditions (and not just the first one)

properties.name: resume

options.name: After Time Interval
options.description: Waits for a certain amount of time

options.name: At Specified Time
options.description: Waits until a specific date and time to continue

options.name: On Webhook Call
options.description: Waits for a webhook call before continuing

options.name: On Form Submitted
options.description: Waits for a form submission before continuing

properties.description: Determines the waiting mode to use before the workflow continues

properties.name: incomingAuthentication
properties.description: If and how incoming resume-webhook-requests to $execution.resumeFormUrl should be authenticated for additional security

properties.name: incomingAuthentication
properties.description: If and how incoming resume-webhook-requests to $execution.resumeUrl should be authenticated for additional security

properties.name: dateTime
properties.description: The date and time to wait for before continuing

properties.name: amount
properties.description: The time to wait

properties.name: amount
properties.description: The time to wait

properties.name: unit
properties.description: The time unit of the Wait Amount value

properties.name: unit
properties.description: The time unit of the Wait Amount value

properties.displayName: The webhook URL will be generated at run time. It can be referenced with the $execution.resumeUrl variable. Send it somewhere before getting to this node. More info
properties.name: webhookNotice

properties.displayName: The form url will be generated at run time. It can be referenced with the $execution.resumeFormUrl variable. Send it somewhere before getting to this node. More info
properties.name: formNotice
formTitle
properties.description: Shown at the top of the form

properties.name: formDescription
properties.placeholder: e.g. We'll get back to you soon
properties.description: Shown underneath the Form Title. Can be used to prompt the user on how to complete the form. Accepts HTML.

values.name: fieldLabel
values.description: Label that appears above the input field

values.name: fieldName
values.description: The name of the field, used in input attributes and referenced by the workflow

values.name: fieldType
values.description: The type of field to add to the form

values.name: elementName
values.description: Optional field. It can be used to include the html in the output.

values.name: placeholder
values.description: Sample text to display inside the field

values.name: defaultValue
values.description: Default value that will be pre-filled in the form field

values.name: defaultValue
values.description: Default date value that will be pre-filled in the form field (format: YYYY-MM-DD)

values.name: defaultValue
values.description: Default value that will be pre-selected. Must match one of the option labels.

values.name: defaultValue
values.description: Default value(s) that will be pre-selected. Must match one or multiple of the option labels. Separate multiple pre-selected options with a comma.

values.name: fieldValue
values.description: Input value can be set here or will be passed as a query parameter via Field Name if no value is set

values.name: fieldOptions
values.description: List of options that can be selected from the dropdown

values.displayName: Multiple Choice is a legacy option, please use Checkboxes or Radio Buttons field type instead
values.name: multiselectLegacyNotice
multiselect
values.description: Whether to allow the user to select multiple options from the dropdown list

values.name: html
values.description: HTML elements to display on the form page
values.hint: Does not accept <script>, <style> or <input> tags

values.name: multipleFiles
values.description: Whether to allow the user to select multiple files from the file input or just one

values.name: acceptFileTypes
values.description: Comma-separated list of allowed file extensions
values.hint: Leave empty to allow all file types

values.displayName: The displayed date is formatted based on the locale of the user's browser
values.name: formatDate
requiredField
values.description: Whether to require the user to enter a value for this field before submitting the form

properties.name: responseMode

options.name: Form Is Submitted
options.description: As soon as this node receives the form submission

options.name: Workflow Finishes
options.description: When the last node of the workflow is executed

options.name: Using 'Respond to Webhook' Node
options.description: When the 'Respond to Webhook' node is executed

properties.description: When to respond to the form submission

properties.name: httpMethod
properties.description: The HTTP method of the Webhook call

properties.name: responseCode
properties.description: The HTTP Response code to return

properties.name: responseMode

options.name: Immediately
options.description: As soon as this node executes

options.name: When Last Node Finishes
options.description: Returns data of the last-executed node

options.name: Using 'Respond to Webhook' Node
options.description: Response defined in that node

properties.description: When and how to respond to the webhook

properties.name: responseData

options.name: All Entries
options.description: Returns all the entries of the last node. Always returns an array.

options.name: First Entry JSON
options.description: Returns the JSON data of the first entry of the last node. Always returns a JSON object.

options.name: First Entry Binary
options.description: Returns the binary data of the first entry of the last node. Always returns a binary file.

options.name: No Response Body
options.description: Returns without a body

properties.description: What data should be returned. If it should return all items as an array or only the first item as object.

properties.name: responseBinaryPropertyName
properties.description: Name of the binary property to return

properties.name: limitWaitTime
properties.description: Whether to limit the time this node should wait for a user response before execution resumes

properties.name: limitType
properties.description: Sets the condition for the execution to resume. Can be a specified date or after some time.

options.name: After Time Interval
options.description: Waits for a certain amount of time

options.name: At Specified Time
options.description: Waits until the set date and time to continue

properties.name: resumeAmount
properties.description: The time to wait

properties.name: resumeUnit
properties.description: Unit of the interval value

properties.name: maxDateAndTime
properties.description: Continue execution after the specified date and time

options.name: binaryData
options.description: Whether the webhook will receive binary data

options.name: binaryPropertyName
options.hint: The name of the output binary field to put the file in
options.description: If the data gets received via "Form-Data Multipart" it will be the prefix and a number starting with 0 will be attached to it

options.name: binaryPropertyName
options.description: The name of the output field to put any binary file data in. Only relevant if binary data is received.

options.name: ignoreBots
options.description: Whether to ignore requests from bots like link previewers and web crawlers

options.name: ipWhitelist
options.description: Comma-separated list of allowed IP addresses. Leave empty to allow all IPs.

options.name: noResponseBody
options.description: Whether to send any body in the response

options.name: rawBody
options.description: Raw body (binary)

options.name: rawBody
options.description: Whether to return the raw body

options.name: responseData
options.description: Custom response data to send

options.name: responseContentType
options.description: Set a custom content-type to return if another one as the "application/json" should be returned

options.name: responseHeaders
options.description: Add headers to the webhook response

values.name: name
values.description: Name of the header

values.name: value
values.description: Value of the header

options.name: responsePropertyName
options.description: Name of the property to return the data of instead of the whole JSON

options.name: webhookSuffix
options.description: This suffix path will be appended to the restart URL. Helpful when using multiple wait nodes.

options.name: appendAttribution
options.description: Whether to include the link “Form automated with workflows” at the bottom of the form

options.name: Form Submitted Text
options.description: Show a response text to the user

options.name: Redirect URL
options.description: Redirect the user to a URL

values.name: formSubmittedText
values.description: The text displayed to users after they fill the form. Leave it empty if don't want to show any additional text.

values.name: redirectUrl
values.description: The URL to redirect users to after they fill the form. Must be a valid URL.

values.name: responseCode

options.description: OK - Request has succeeded
Created - Request has been fulfilled
No Content - Request processed, no content returned
Moved Permanently - Requested resource moved permanently
Found - Requested resource moved temporarily
Not Modified - Resource has not been modified
Bad Request - Request could not be understood
Unauthorized - Request requires user authentication
Forbidden - Server understood, but refuses to fulfill
Not Found - Server has not found a match

options.name: Custom Code
options.description: Write any HTTP code

values.description: The HTTP response code to return
